name: Chicken in Coconut Milk Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 quarter-sized pieces dried galangal, also known as Siamese Ginger, or twice as much fresh galangal
4 stalks fresh lemon grass, bruised and cut into 2-inch lengths
1 large whole boned chicken (about 1 lb), breast cut into 1/2 inch pieces
3-4 fresh serrano chiles, sliced into rounds
recipeInstructions: In Thai, this is called Gai Tom Kha. Be sure to use unsweetened coconut milk, which is available in Asian markets. In a saucepan, bring the coconut milk, chicken stock, galangal and lemon grass to a boil; reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes. Strain the stock; discard galangal and lemon grass. Return the stock to a boil, add the chicken and reduce to a simmer until tender (about 2 minutes). Add fish sauce, citrus leaves and chiles. Stir in lime juice. Garnish with more chiles to taste and coriander leaves. Serve hot.
